title: Mermaid diagram utility functions for direction toggling and node extraction
purpose: Provides utility functions for detecting and toggling diagram directions, extracting node and edge information from DOM elements, and building Mermaid node definitions and styles.
module_context: Exports type definitions for diagram direction and node types, a constant mapping of node types to their shapes and styles, and functions for manipulating Mermaid diagram content and extracting information from DOM elements.

steps.content: Validate that the input content is a non-empty string.
Match the content against a regex pattern to detect diagram direction.
IF a match is found, return the detected direction in uppercase.
ELSE return null indicating no direction was detected.
Validate that the input content is a non-empty string.
Detect the current direction of the diagram.
Determine the new direction based on the pairing of LR/TD or RL/BT.
IF a direction is found, replace it in the content with the new direction.
ELSE add the new direction after the diagram type in the content.
Return an object containing the updated content, old direction, and new direction.
Take the input string and escape all special regex characters.
Return the escaped string.
Start walking up the DOM tree from the given element.
Check for a data-id attribute on the current element.
IF a data-id is found, return it immediately.
ELSE check for an id attribute that starts with flowchart-.
IF an id matches the older format, extract and return the node ID.
ELSE continue walking up the DOM tree until a match is found or root is reached.
IF no match is found, return null.

methods.name: extractEdgeInfo
methods.normalized_params: (element: Element): { source: string; target: string; label?: string } | null

steps.content: Start walking up the DOM tree from the given element.
Check if the current element has the class edgePath.
IF the class is found, extract the edge ID from the id or data-id attribute.
Parse the edge ID to extract source and target node IDs.
IF parsing is successful, return an object with source, target, and optional label.
ELSE continue walking up the DOM tree until a match is found or root is reached.
IF no match is found, return null.
Split the content into lines.
Construct a regex pattern to match the node ID followed by shape characters.
Iterate through each line to find a match for the pattern.
IF a match is found, return the 1-indexed line number.
ELSE return null indicating the node was not found.

methods.name: findEdgeLine
methods.normalized_params: (source: string, target: string, content: string): number | null

steps.content: Split the content into lines.
Construct a regex pattern to match source and target node IDs with connection arrows.
Iterate through each line to find a match for the pattern.
IF a match is found, return the 1-indexed line number.
ELSE return null indicating the edge was not found.
Find all existing node IDs in the content using a regex pattern.
Store the existing IDs in a set for fast lookup.
Start with a counter of 1 and increment until a unique ID is found.
Return the unique node ID constructed from the prefix and counter.

methods.name: buildNodeDefinition
methods.normalized_params: (id: string, label: string, type: NodeType['name']): string

steps.content: Retrieve the node type definition from the NODE_TYPES constant.
Construct and return a Mermaid node definition string using the ID, label, and shape characters.
Retrieve the node type definition from the NODE_TYPES constant.
Construct and return a Mermaid style statement string using the ID and style.
